title: Permanent Portfolio: A Cross-Asset Allocation Study
subtitle: NASDAQ · S&P 500 · Gold · CSI Dividend Index (A-Shares)
abstract: We examine equal-weighted permanent portfolio performance (25% each) across four major global assets over 21.2 years. Annual rebalancing delivers CAGR 11.93% with Sharpe 0.75, outperforming buy-and-hold and higher-frequency strategies. Maximum drawdown is capped at −40.4%, versus −70.2% for the most volatile single constituent.

title: Beyond Equal Weight: Nine Allocation Strategies Compared
subtitle: Momentum · Risk Parity · Trend Following · Max Sharpe · Min Variance
abstract: We test eight alternative allocation strategies against an equal-weight permanent portfolio baseline across four global assets (2005–2026). Max Sharpe optimization delivers the highest CAGR (13.76%) and Sharpe ratio (0.88), outperforming the baseline by +183bps annually. Risk Parity and Min Variance reduce drawdown below −38% while preserving near-baseline returns. Trend-following approaches underperform on monthly data.
